label: Primary text unit label, e.g. name of the main text
summary: A summary of the text unit content. This is a medium-length prose description that provides an overview of what this text unit contains, its language, and any notable features.
locus: Locus of the text unit, e.g. ff. 1r-85v

work_wit.locus: Locus of this work witness, e.g. ff. 1r-45v
work_wit.alt_title: Witness-specific alternate title, e.g. title as given in this manuscript
work_wit.as_written: Title or incipit as written in the manuscript, in the original script

excerpt.locus: Locus of the heading, e.g. f. 1r
excerpt.as_written: Heading text as written in the original script
translation: English translation of the heading text
note: A note about this heading, e.g. decorative heading in red ink

excerpt.locus: Locus of the incipit, e.g. f. 1v
excerpt.as_written: Incipit text as written in the original script
translation: English translation of the incipit text
note: A note about the incipit, e.g. standard opening formula

excerpt.locus: Locus of the first preserved line, e.g. f. 3r
excerpt.as_written: First preserved line as written in the original script
translation: English translation of the first preserved line
note: A note explaining why the text begins imperfectly, e.g. folio lost

excerpt.locus: Locus of the quoted passage, e.g. f. 20v
excerpt.as_written: Quoted passage as written in the original script
translation: English translation of the quoted passage
note: A note identifying the source of the quotation

excerpt.locus: Locus of the subscription, e.g. f. 85v
excerpt.as_written: Subscription text as written in the original script
note: A note about the subscription, e.g. colophon in red ink

excerpt.locus: Locus of the explicit, e.g. f. 85v
excerpt.as_written: Explicit text as written in the original script
note: A note about the explicit

excerpt.locus: Locus of the last preserved line, e.g. f. 85v
excerpt.as_written: Last preserved line as written in the original script
translation: English translation of the last preserved line
note: A note explaining why the text ends imperfectly, e.g. remainder lost

excerpt.locus: Locus of the prologue, e.g. f. 1r
excerpt.as_written: Prologue text as written in the original script
note: A note about the prologue

contents.label: Section label, e.g. Chapter 1 — short contents entry
Section label with a linked work record, e.g. Part 1
note: A note about this section, e.g. marginalia or corrections present

contents.label: Section label — third contents entry to show repeatability

note: A note about this work witness, e.g. translation tradition or textual affiliation.
A second note about this work witness to show the note field is repeatable.

bib.citation: Full bibliographic citation string for a reference work cited for this work witness.
Full bibliographic citation string. Second bib entry to show repeatability.

work.desc_title: Descriptive title for an unidentified or unlinked work, e.g. Unidentified liturgical text

work_wit.locus: Locus of this second work witness, e.g. ff. 86r-90v
work_wit.as_written: Title or incipit as written for the second work witness

excerpt.as_written: Incipit text of the second work witness
translation: English translation of the second work witness incipit

note: A note about this desc_title work witness, showing the unlinked work pattern.

para.locus: Locus of the paracontent item, e.g. f. 1r
para.as_written: Paracontent text as written in the original script
translation: English translation of the paracontent text

assoc_name.as_written: Name as written in the paracontent
note: A note about this associated name in the paracontent

assoc_place.as_written: Place name as written in the paracontent
note: A note about this associated place in the paracontent

assoc_date.value: Date as written in the paracontent, e.g. 10th c. CE
note: A note about this date, e.g. derived from colophon
A note about this paracontent item, e.g. rubric in red ink.
A second note to show the note field is repeatable.

para.locus: Locus of the colophon, e.g. f. 85v
para.as_written: Colophon text as written in the original script
translation: English translation of the colophon text

assoc_name.as_written: Scribe name as written in the colophon
note: Named in the colophon as the copyist

assoc_name.value: Patron name as a free-text value when not linked to an agent record
note: Referenced in the colophon but not linked to an agent record

assoc_place.as_written: Place name as written in the colophon
note: Named in the colophon as the place of production

assoc_date.value: Origin date as expressed in the colophon, e.g. 10th c. CE
note: Date derived from the colophon; confirmed by paleographic dating
A note about this colophon, e.g. partially damaged; final lines illegible.

note.value: This is a contents note. It describes the textual contents of the text unit in prose form. It can be a short phrase or a full paragraph.
This is a general note. It can contain any additional information about the text unit not covered by other fields. The note field is repeatable.
This is a second general note to show that multiple notes of the same type are allowed.

bib.citation: Full bibliographic citation string for a reference work cited at the text unit level.
bib.alt_shelf: [Alternate shelfmark from an older catalog, e.g. 652]
note: A note about this bibliographic entry

bib.citation: Full bibliographic citation string. Second bib entry at text unit level to show repeatability.

program.label: Description program name, e.g. Sinai Arabic Manuscripts Project
program.description: Full description of the program under which this record was created, including dates, funders, and project goals. This can be a long paragraph.

desc_provenance.rights: Rights statement for the descriptive metadata.

cataloguer.message: Cataloguer message describing what was done in this edit, e.g. initial description or migration from legacy system.
Second cataloguer entry to show the field is repeatable. Describes a subsequent update.
